biases_detected: shell_tool_preference_violations:RECURRING:STABLE — rate ~62.5% (10/16 tool-using units), down from peak 87.5% but above the one-cycle 50% low; subvariants: ls-for-directory-listing in 4b1b8967-1 (ls -la *.xlsx), 4b1b8967-25 (ls -la /tmp/pt_sweep_1000rows_*.json), 4b1b8967-27 (ls frontend/docs/tech/backend/ai-agents/, ls backend/docs/), 6ec6743c-5 (ls -d ../extractor-webserver, ls ../extractor-webserver, ls -la automation/), 6ec6743c-14 (ls -la preview-job-output/, ls src/core/widgets-v2/, ls src/core/widgets-v2/docs/); ls-t-for-timestamp-sort in 4b1b8967-19 (two instances J=$(ls -t /tmp/pt_sweep_3rows_*.json | head -1)), 4b1b8967-20, 4b1b8967-25, 4b1b8967-26 (three separate J=$(ls -t ...) calls) — confirmed stable across consecutive units as documented in prior cycles; sed-instead-of-read-with-offset in 4b1b8967-19 (sed -n '1,60p' lib/tasks/errors.py | head -60), 6ec6743c-14 (sed -n '1,80p' + cat | head -80, sed -n '91,100p'); cat-instead-of-read in 6ec6743c-22 (cat on task JSONL output, first attempt failed then adapted), 6ec6743c-23 (cat /tmp/task2-modal-review.md immediately after Write tool wrote that file); clean units: 4b1b8967-2, 4b1b8967-8 (Python-only Bash), 4b1b8967-32 (rg+Read), 4b1b8967-33 (git ops only), 4b1b8967-38 (rg only), 6ec6743c-17 (atone scripts using rg correctly)

grep_in_compound_bash:RECURRING — present in 2 units: 6ec6743c-5 (grep -niE 'output|whiteboard|cache|\.claude' .gitignore embedded mid-compound Bash string), 6ec6743c-14 (grep -iv 'preview-job-output.modal' and grep -i 'delete' in pipe chains); hook architecture gap (argv[0]-only interception) remains the structural cause and is unaddressed; seventh or more consecutive RECURRING cycle with zero improvement and no hook fix deployed

document_format_anchoring:RECURRING — unit 4b1b8967-27 (outcome=Corrected) wrote a long engineering-oriented model evaluation document when user input (165 chars, topic_keywords: ['apply','changes','recommend','gemmini','flash']) contained no audience specification; unit 4b1b8967-28 (is_correction=true, outcome=Accepted) accepted the framing correction cleanly; pattern was flagged NEW in the immediately prior cycle and is now confirmed RECURRING for the second consecutive cycle with a direct correction event; agent failed to identify audience register before writing

scope_expansion:RECURRING:S3_CONFIRMED — unit 6ec6743c-17 (outcome=Corrected) is the speculative-abstractions-without-a-load-bearing-caller S3 event already logged in prior cycle analyses; agent built deriveJobOutputStats + 15-field JobOutputStats view-model when user asked only for raw aggregate data and explicitly reserved the UI layer; atone correctly filed with RCA and runnable precheck procedure; no new S3 scope events in the remaining 23 units this batch; third confirmed S3 recurrence of this slug across the full analysis history
cross_references: 4b1b8967-33: user sent 36-char message with topic_keywords ['push','code'] — this is an explicit named push request, not a terse continuation signal; agent correctly ran verification triad (git status + log + diff) before committing and pushing; outcome Accepted; CONFIRMS the dream insight boundary rule: the user named the external action explicitly, making this an authorized crossing of the local/external boundary rather than an inferred one from a continuation like 'ahead' or 'next'; this is the correct discriminant the dream insight describes

6ec6743c-17: scope_expansion S3 event directly violates the dream insight that terse-input signals should authorize local work only; the user's 143-char input ('deleted the code you wrote for me for the status, you just reinvented the wheel for complexity I DID NOT FUCKING ASK FOR') is the canonical over-scope user anger signal; the pre-task user message was likely similarly short and did not specify a view-model design; the agent inferred a presentation-shaped deliverable from a data-retrieval request, which matches the dream insight pattern of over-extending scope from execution signals

4b1b8967-27: document_format_anchoring failure is adjacent to the dream insight about audience-aware execution; the 165-char user input specifying 'apply changes recommend gemmini flash' did not specify document audience; the agent defaulted to engineering register without a one-line audience clarification; the dream insight about 'two plausible readings of intent where picking wrong means redoing work' is precisely this case — an audience check (10 seconds) would have prevented the full write-then-correct cycle
recommendations: shell_tool_preference_violations: nine consecutive cycles of RECURRING with rate oscillating 50-87.5% and no sustained improvement; advisory tracking is structurally incapable of resolving this — the corrective mechanism is advisory text in a context that is not re-read mid-session; requires a PreToolUse hook that (1) blocks ls used as a directory lister when LS/Glob tools are available, (2) warns on J=$(ls -t ... | head -1) patterns offering a glob+stat alternative, (3) warns on sed -n 'L1,L2p' file suggesting Read with offset/limit; do not add further advisory text to CLAUDE.md or atone entries for this pattern

grep_in_compound_bash: root cause is hook architecture (argv[0]-only interception makes mid-pipeline grep invisible); the fix is compound-string parsing in the hook or a post-Bash tool check that scans the command string for grep not at position 0; advisory flagging has produced zero improvement across 7+ cycles; deprioritize further advisory entries and file a hook-design proposal

document_format_anchoring: two confirmed correction cycles; add a UserPromptSubmit hinter that fires when write/draft/report keywords appear in a prompt lacking explicit audience specification ('for sales', 'executive summary', 'engineering doc', 'for X audience'); the hinter should prompt a one-line audience check before writing begins; this is a cheap gate that prevents the full write-then-correct cycle

scope_expansion: the existing runnable precheck procedure from the RCA is the correct enforcement mechanism; verify the precheck prompt fires during PartTypeClassifier-style 'just get me the data' requests in future sessions; no new advisory text is productive — the S3 atone + RCA already encodes the correct behavior; monitor for fourth S3 recurrence
